
### double-frees 

```{.c code-line-numbers=true}
{{< include /overflow-heap/double-free-code.c >}}
```

::: {.r-stack}

![](/overflow-heap/texfig/dblFree.figure-1.svg){.overlayfigtr height=600px}

:::

### double-frees 

```{.c code-line-numbers="1"}
{{< include /overflow-heap/double-free-code.c >}}
```

::: {.r-stack}


![](/overflow-heap/texfig/dblFree.figure-2.svg){.overlayfigtr height=600px}

:::

### double-frees 

```{.c code-line-numbers="2"}
{{< include /overflow-heap/double-free-code.c >}}
```

::: {.r-stack}


![](/overflow-heap/texfig/dblFree.figure-2.svg){.overlayfigtr height=600px}

:::

### double-frees 

```{.c code-line-numbers="3"}
{{< include /overflow-heap/double-free-code.c >}}
```

::: {.r-stack}

![](/overflow-heap/texfig/dblFree.figure-3.svg){.overlayfigtr height=600px}

:::

::: {.myoverbox .vlow .left .fragment}

malloc returns something _still on free list_ <br />
because double-free made loop in the linked list

:::


### double-frees 

```{.c code-line-numbers="4-12"}
{{< include /overflow-heap/double-free-code.c >}}
```

::: {.r-stack}

![](/overflow-heap/texfig/dblFree.figure-3.svg){.overlayfigtr height=600px}

:::

::: {.myoverbox .vlow .left .fragment}

attacker can overwrite next pointer <br/> controls where future malloc() goes

:::

### double-free expansion 

```{.c code-line-numbers=true}
{{< include /overflow-heap/double-free-code-expand.c >}}
```

::: {.r-stack}
![](/overflow-heap/texfig/dblFreeExpand.figure-1.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=1 .overlayfigtr height=400px}

![](/overflow-heap/texfig/dblFreeExpand.figure-2.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=2 .overlayfigtr height=400px}

![](/overflow-heap/texfig/dblFreeExpand.figure-3.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=3 .overlayfigtr height=400px}

![](/overflow-heap/texfig/dblFreeExpand.figure-4.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=4 .overlayfigtr height=400px}

![](/overflow-heap/texfig/dblFreeExpand.figure-5.svg){.fragment .fade-in-then-out fragment-index=5 .overlayfigtr height=400px}


:::


### double-free notes  {.smaller}


* this attack has apparently not been possible for a while
* most malloc/new's <em>check for double-frees</em> explicitly 
   * (e.g., look for a bit in <code>size</code> data)
* prevents this issue --- also catches programmer errors
* pretty cheap
